title: Quantum effect could power the next generation of battery-free devices
why:
- Researchers demonstrated that the nonlinear Hall effect (NLHE), a quantum phenomenon in topological materials, enables direct conversion of alternating electrical signals into direct current without magnetic fields or traditional diodes.
- The NLHE remains stable up to room temperature, making it practical for real-world applications.
- The generated voltage in the material can be tuned in direction and strength by temperature, due to interactions between imperfections and crystal lattice vibrations.
- This effect could enable the development of battery-free, energy-harvesting devices such as self-powered sensors and ultra-fast components for next-generation wireless technology.
